title: cyperus litoreus
intro: Cyperus litoreus, also known as sea flats sedge, is a plant species in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is commonly found in coastal areas and wetlands. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its roots and rhizomes. The health benefits of Cyperus litoreus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce swelling. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are made by steeping the dried roots and rhizomes in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
